summarization.a: The line chart illustrates a non-linear  increase in lithium concentration (in at.%) as the number of LiO<sup>t</sup>Bu + H<sub>2</sub>O sub-cycles increases.

data_extraction.a:
| Number of LiO<sup>t</sup>Bu + H<sub>2</sub>O sub-cycles | Lithium concentration (at.%) |
|---|---|
| 0 | 0 |
| 1 | 19.67 |
| 3 | 21.06 |

a.question: Does the lithium concentration depend on the number of  Lithium tert-butoxide linearly?
After which sub-cycle does the surface become saturated with Li?
Based on this graph, is it efficient to use multiple consecutive lithium precursor sub-cycles to increase the lithium content in the film? Why?
a.answer: No, it is not efficient. Based on the plot, the lithium concentration saturates after the first cycle. Adding more consecutive lithium pulses wastes time and precursor without significantly changing the film composition.

a.question: If the process did not exhibit self-limiting behavior on the Lithium-covered surface, how would this curve look like instead?
a.answer: The curve would show a linear, steady increase in concentration.
